body:
## Summary

When using `$ --isolated screen --detached --session <custom-name>`, two issues prevent reliable session tracking:

1. **`$ --status <custom-name>` fails** — The `$` CLI generates its own internal UUID but `--status` only looks up by that internal UUID, not by the `--session` name.
2. **`$ --detached` reports immediate completion** — Status shows `executed` with `exitCode: 0` immediately because it tracks the wrapper process, not the actual screen session.

## Reproduction

```bash
# Start a long-running command with custom session name
$ --isolated screen --detached --session my-custom-session -- sleep 60

# Try to check status by custom session name → FAILS
$ --status my-custom-session
# Error: No execution found with UUID: my-custom-session

# Check by internal UUID from output → shows "executed" immediately (wrong)
$ --status <internal-uuid-from-output>
# status: executed
# exitCode: 0
# duration: ~0.08s  (clearly just the wrapper, not the sleep)

# But the screen session is actually still running
screen -r my-custom-session
# (attached to running session)
```

## Expected Behavior

1. `$ --status my-custom-session` should find the execution created with `--session my-custom-session`
2. When `--isolated screen --detached` is used, the status should reflect whether the screen session is still running (not just whether the `$` wrapper exited)

## Workaround

In [link-assistant/hive-mind](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind), we've implemented two workarounds:
1. Extract the internal UUID from `$` output and use that for `--status` queries
2. Fall back to `screen -ls` to check if the screen session is still active

See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1545

## Suggested Fixes

### For issue 1: Support --session name lookups in --status

When an execution is created with `--session <name>`, store a mapping from that name to the internal UUID. Then `$ --status <name>` should resolve to the correct execution.

### For issue 2: Track screen session lifecycle for --detached mode

When `--isolated screen --detached` is used, instead of marking the execution as `executed` when the wrapper exits, periodically check (or on `--status` query) whether the named screen session is still running via `screen -ls`.

## Environment

- start-command version: installed globally via npm
- Node.js: v20.20.2
- Platform: linux (Docker container)
- Related: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1545
title: Bug: --session name not usable with --status, and --detached reports immediate completion
